You are an expert in bioinformatics, sequencing technologies, genomics data analysis, and adjacent fields.

You are given findings from a MultiQC report, generated by a bioinformatics workflow.
MultiQC supports various bioinformatics tools that output QC metrics, and aggregates those metrics
into a single report. It outputs a "General Statistics" table with key metrics for each sample across
all tools. That table is followed by more detailed sections from specific tools, that can include tables,
as well as plots of different types (bar plot, line plot, scatter plot, heatmap, etc.)

You are given data from such a report. Your task is to analyse the data, and
give 1-2 bullet points of a very short and concise overall summary for the results.
Don't waste words: mention only the important QC issues. If there are no issues, just say so.
Just print one or two bullet points, nothing else.
Please do not add any extra headers to the response.

Use markdown to format your reponse for readability. Use directives with pre-defined classes
.text-green, .text-red, and .text-yellow to highlight severity, e.g. :span[39.2%]{.text-red}.
Highlight any mentioned sample names or sample named prefixes or suffixes with a sample directive,
and make sure to use the same color classes for severity, e.g. :sample[A1001.2003]{.text-yellow}
or :sample[A1001]{.text-yellow}. Do not put multiple sample names inside one directive.

You must use only multiples of 4 spaces to indent nested lists.



Two examples of short summaries:

- :span[11/13 samples]{.text-green} show consistent metrics within expected ranges.
- :sample[A1001.2003]{.text-red} and :sample[A1001.2004]{.text-red} exhibit extremely high percentage of :span[duplicates]{.text-red} (:span[65.54%]{.text-red} and :span[83.14%]{.text-red}, respectively).

- All samples show good quality metrics with :span[75.7-77.0%]{.text-green} CpG methylation and :span[76.3-86.0%]{.text-green} alignment rates
- :sample[2wk]{.text-yellow} samples show slightly higher duplication (:span[11-15%]{.text-yellow}) compared to :sample[1wk]{.text-green} samples (:span[6-9%]{.text-green})'



----------------------

Tools used in the report:

1. STAR
Description: <p>Universal RNA-seq aligner.</p>
Links: https://github.com/alexdobin/STAR

----------------------

2. Bowtie 2 / HiSAT2
Description: <p>Results from both Bowtie 2 and HISAT2, tools for aligning reads against a reference genome.</p>
Links: http://bowtie-bio.sourceforge.net/bowtie2/, https://ccb.jhu.edu/software/hisat2/

----------------------

3. BCL Convert
Description: <p>Demultiplexes data and converts BCL files to FASTQ file formats for downstream analysis.</p>
Links: https://support.illumina.com/sequencing/sequencing_software/bcl-convert.html

----------------------

----------------------

Tool: STAR
Section: Summary Statistics
Section description: Summary statistics from the STAR alignment
Title: STAR: Summary Statistics

Plot type: violin plot

Number of samples: 9

Metrics:
Total reads - Number of input reads
Aligned - Mapped reads
Aligned - % Mapped reads
Uniq aligned - Uniquely mapped reads
Uniq aligned - % Uniquely mapped reads
Multimapped - Multiple mapped reads
Avg. read len - Average input read length
Avg. mapped len - Average mapped length
Splices - Number of splices: Total
Annotated splices - Number of splices: Annotated (sjdb)
GT/AG splices - Number of splices: GT/AG
GC/AG splices - Number of splices: GC/AG
AT/AC splices - Number of splices: AT/AC
Non-canonical splices - Number of splices: Non-canonical
Mismatch rate - Mismatch rate per base
Del rate - Deletion rate per base
Del len - Deletion average length
Ins rate - Insertion rate per base
Ins len - Insertion average length

|Sample Name|Total reads|Aligned|Aligned|Uniq aligned|Uniq aligned|Multimapped|Avg. read len|Avg. mapped len|Splices|Annotated splices|GT/AG splices|GC/AG splices|AT/AC splices|Non-canonical splices|Mismatch rate|Del rate|Del len|Ins rate|Ins len|

----------------------

Tool: Bowtie 2 / HiSAT2
Section: Paired-end alignments
Section description: This plot shows the number of reads aligning to the reference in different ways.
                <div class="alert alert-warning">Please note that single mate alignment counts are halved to tally with pair counts properly.</div>
Section help text: There are 6 possible types of alignment:
<ul>
<li><strong>PE mapped uniquely</strong>: Pair has only one occurence in the reference genome.</li>
<li><strong>PE mapped discordantly uniquely</strong>: Pair has only one occurence but not in proper pair.</li>
<li><strong>PE one mate mapped uniquely</strong>: One read of a pair has one occurence.</li>
<li><strong>PE multimapped</strong>: Pair has multiple occurence.</li>
<li><strong>PE one mate multimapped</strong>: One read of a pair has multiple occurence.</li>
<li><strong>PE neither mate aligned</strong>: Pair has no occurence.</li>
</ul>
Title: Bowtie 2: PE Alignment Scores

Plot type: bar plot
Values: # Reads

|Sample|PE neither mate aligned|
|---|---|
|13AIRCJF|12,646,155|
|12AIRCJF|12,571,110|
|11AIRCJF|13,786,127|
|10AIRCJF|14,498,847|
|6CIECJF|13,623,349|
|5CIECJF|12,451,226|
|4CIECJF|13,912,561|
|3CIECJF|12,814,262|

----------------------

Tool: BCL Convert
Section: Sample Statistics
Section description: Statistics about each sample for each flowcell
Title: bclconvert Sample Statistics

Plot type: violin plot

Number of samples: 8

Metrics:
Clusters - Total number of clusters (read pairs) for this sample as determined by bclconvert demultiplexing (millions)
Yield - Total number of bases for this sample as determined by bclconvert demultiplexing (millions)
Clusters/run - Percentage of clusters (read pairs) for this sample in this run, as determined by bclconvert demultiplexing
Yield/run - Percentage of sequenced bases for this sample in this run
Bases ≥ Q30 (PF) - Number of bases with a Phred score of 30 or higher, passing filter (millions)
Bases ≥ Q30 (PF) - Percent of bases with a Phred score of 30 or higher, passing filter (millions)
Perfect index - Percent of reads with perfect index (0 mismatches)
One mismatch index - Percent of reads with one mismatch index
Mean quality score - Mean quality score of bases
Index - Sample index
Project - Sample project

|Sample Name|Clusters|Yield|Clusters/run|Yield/run|Bases ≥ Q30 (PF)|Bases ≥ Q30 (PF)|Perfect index|One mismatch index|Mean quality score|Index|Project|
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
|10AIRCJFUNIFESP (L1)|29.00|5857.53|13.64|13.64|5238.82|89.44|89.02|10.98|37.95|ATATGCATGT-CCAGGCACCA||
|11AIRCJFUNIFESP (L1)|27.57|5569.60|12.97|12.97|5039.19|90.48|87.05|12.95|38.16|CTAGCTTCAA-AGCTTACACA||
|12AIRCJFUNIFESP (L1)|25.14|5078.73|11.83|11.83|4621.52|91.00|73.58|26.42|38.27|TCCGTTATGT-TACACTACAA||
|13AIRCJFUNIFESP (L1)|25.29|5109.05|11.90|11.90|4611.26|90.26|88.61|11.39|38.12|GGTCTATTAA-CGTGTCGTGG||
|3CIECJFUNIFESP (L1)|25.63|5176.96|12.05|12.05|4693.34|90.66|89.41|10.59|38.20|TGCCGGTCAG-TTGTATCAGG||
|4CIECJFUNIFESP (L1)|27.83|5620.67|13.09|13.09|5100.76|90.75|87.33|12.67|38.22|CACTCAATTC-CACAACTTAA||
|5CIECJFUNIFESP (L1)|24.90|5030.30|11.71|11.71|4537.70|90.21|68.22|31.78|38.11|TCTCACACGC-TCACTGTCCG||
|6CIECJFUNIFESP (L1)|27.25|5503.83|12.82|12.82|4979.86|90.48|90.67|9.33|38.16|TCAATGGAGA-GTTGTAGTGC||

Tool: BCL Convert
Section: Lane Statistics
Section description: Statistics about each lane for each flowcell
Title: bclconvert Lane Statistics

Plot type: violin plot

Number of samples: 9

Metrics:
Clusters - Total number of clusters (read pairs) for this sample as determined by bclconvert demultiplexing (millions)
Yield - Total number of bases for this sample as determined by bclconvert demultiplexing (millions)
Bases ≥ Q30 (PF) - Percent of bases with a Phred score of 30 or higher, passing filter
Perfect index - Reads with perfect index - 0 mismatches (millions)
One mismatch - Reads with one mismatch index (millions)
Perfect index - Percent of reads with perfect index - 0 mismatches
One mismatch - Percent of reads with one mismatch
Mean quality score - Mean quality score of bases

|Run ID - Lane|Clusters|Yield|Bases ≥ Q30 (PF)|Perfect index|One mismatch|Perfect index|One mismatch|Mean quality score|
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
|L1|212.61|42946.67|90.40|179.63|32.97|84.49|15.51|38.15|

----------------------

Tool: BCL Convert
Section: Clusters by lane
Section description: Number of reads per lane (with number of perfect index reads).
                <div class="alert alert-warning">
                    <strong>Warning:</strong> Found multiple runs from the same sequencer output.
                    Runs were merged and <em>Undetermined Reads</em> were recalculated.
                </div>
Section help text: Perfect index reads are those that do not have a single mismatch.
                All samples of a lane are combined. Undetermined reads are treated as a third category.
Title: bclconvert: Clusters by lane

Plot type: bar plot
Values: Number of clusters

|Sample|Perfect Index Reads|Mismatched Index Reads|Undetermined Reads|
|---|---|---|---|
|L1|179,633,078|32,974,196|nan|

----------------------

Tool: BCL Convert
Section: Clusters by sample
Section description: Number of reads per sample.
Section help text: Perfect index reads are those that do not have a single mismatch.
                All samples are aggregated across lanes combined. Undetermined reads are ignored.
                Undetermined reads are treated as a separate sample.
Title: bclconvert: Clusters by sample

Plot type: bar plot
Values: Number of clusters

### Index mismatches

|Sample|Perfect Index Reads|Mismatched Index Reads|
|---|---|---|
|13AIRCJFUNIFESP|22,410,532|2,881,778|
|12AIRCJFUNIFESP|18,498,556|6,643,664|

|4CIECJFUNIFESP|24,300,864|3,524,258|
|3CIECJFUNIFESP|22,913,778|2,714,746|
